description_long: This chaise features a modern, minimalist design with a geometric L-shaped profile, constructed from a single block-like form. The surface is smooth and finished in a uniform metallic gold color, giving it a sleek and contemporary appearance. The backrest is tall and straight, seamlessly integrated into the main body without any visible segmentation or decorative elements. There are no visible armrests, legs, or additional structural supports, emphasizing its monolithic and sculptural quality. The upholstery appears solid and untextured, with no visible seams, tufting, or patterns. The overall form suggests a sturdy, functional piece suitable for urban public spaces such as parks or plazas.
description: A sleek, gold, L-shaped chaise with a tall, integrated backrest and a smooth, untextured surface, designed in a minimalist, monolithic style.
description_view_0: From this view, the chaise appears as a tall, rectangular block with a flat top and no visible features except its smooth gold surface.
description_view_1: This side view reveals the L-shaped profile, showing the extended seat and the tall, straight backrest at one end.
description_view_2: From the rear, the chaise displays two stacked rectangular sections, highlighting the depth and the backrest's height.
description_view_3: This opposite side view mirrors view 1, again showing the L-shaped form with the backrest on the right side.
attribute_backrest_design: The backrest is tall, straight, and seamlessly integrated into the main body, with no visible curvature, segmentation, or decorative features.
attribute_armrest_presence: No armrests are present on either side of the chaise.
attribute_leg_style: There are no visible legs; the chaise sits directly on the ground as a solid block.
attribute_upholstery_pattern: The surface is solid gold in color, smooth, and untextured, with no visible patterns, tufting, or seams.
attribute_cushion_arrangement: No separate cushions are visible; the seating and backrest surfaces are continuous and integrated into the main structure.
